com.mentor.chs.plugin.useraccess

Interface IXUserAccessibilityEvaluator<O extends IXObject>

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXEvaluator

    public interface IXUserAccessibilityEvaluator<O extends IXObject>
extends IXEvaluator
    All custom user access filters needs to implement this interface. User Accessibility Evaluation plugin (IXUserAccessibilityEvaluation) implementation will return a set of filters which can contain filter of type IXUserAccessibilityEvaluator. It will be used by the application to restrict user access

    Sample usage of the implementation class: 

          Sample user access filter to to restrict a project P1 to a user called Pat
 public class DemoProjectFilter implements IXUserAccessibilityEvaluator<IXProject>
 {
                public void apply(IXUser currentUser, Collection<IXUserAccessibilityResult<IXProject>> ixUserAccessibilityResults)
        {
                        if("pat".equalsIgnoreCase(currentUser.getUserName()))
             {
                                for(IXUserAccessibilityResult<IXProject> accessibilityResult : ixUserAccessibilityResults)
                {
                                        if("P1".equalsIgnoreCase(accessibilityResult.getObject().getAttribute("name")))
                    {
                                                accessibilityResult.setAccessibility(UserAccessibility.NOACCESS);
                                                break;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
 }

    See Also:
    IXUserAccessibilityEvaluation, IXEvaluation

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void apply(IXUser currentUser, java.util.Collection<IXUserAccessibilityResult<O>> results)
      This will be invoked by Capital Application whenever user attempts to edit/read the specified object.

    • Method Detail

      • apply

        void apply(IXUser currentUser,
           java.util.Collection<IXUserAccessibilityResult<O>> results)
        This will be invoked by Capital Application whenever user attempts to edit/read the specified object.
        Parameters:
        results. - This collection of objects would be filled in by Capital and passed to plugin. Plugin needs to check the accessibility of individual objects from the collection and set its accessibility level into the objects which will be used by Capital to restrict its access to current user.
        currentUser. - The current logged in user
